entries.label: Version 1
entries.prompt:
Recreate the same solutions template page layout used for "Crypto Commerce":
1. Hero card (rounded, soft accent-tinted background): back link "← Back to solutions", small uppercase eyebrow label, large bold H1 headline, one-paragraph subtext.
2. "The Risk" section (two columns, light grey background): left has label "THE RISK", a large bold stat, and a short paragraph; right has a rounded accent-tinted callout card with a short bold centered uppercase phrase.
3. "Key benefits" section (white background): H2 "Key benefits", grid of rounded light-grey cards (2 per row), each with a bold title and short description.
4. Closing CTA section (light grey background): centered H2 question with a solid accent-colored button beneath.
5. Footer: centered copyright line.

Content to use:
- Eyebrow label: HIGH-VALUE OTC TRANSFERS
- H1: Reduce settlement risk for institutional transfers.
- Subtext: Wallet Nation enables OTC desks to protect high-value transfers with both counterparties covered within the same settlement window, without introducing custodial risk.
- Risk label: THE RISK
- Risk stat: One-sided risk
- Risk paragraph: High-value OTC transfers often expose one counterparty to settlement risk before the other performs. Wallet Nation removes that imbalance with conditional settlement that protects both sides simultaneously.
- Callout box text: MUTUAL SETTLEMENT WINDOW
- Key benefit 1 — Counterparty protection: Both parties in an OTC transfer are protected within the same settlement window — neither is exposed first.
- Key benefit 2 — Institutional-grade control: Settlement stays conditional on agreed terms, enforced through cryptographic credentials rather than trust alone.
- Key benefit 3 — No custodial exposure: Wallet Nation never holds the transferred assets, preserving the self-custody principles institutional counterparties expect.
- CTA headline: Ready to protect your OTC transfers?
- Button text: Talk to us
